steps.stuck: The back half of this room is invisible — keep the Lens of Truth held (it drains magic) to see the platforms and the chest. Use the Longshot to cross gaps you can't reach.

steps.t: Fire Trial: wear the Goron Tunic so the heat doesn't hurt you, then use the Hover Boots to keep the central platform from sinking as you cross the lava. Lift the huge pillar with the Golden Gauntlets, gather the Silver Rupees to open the way, then Light-Arrow the orb to free the Fire Sage, Darunia.
steps.stuck: No Goron Tunic means the heat ticks away your hearts — equip it before stepping in. Keep the Hover Boots on so the sinking platform holds you up, and don't pause between hops.

steps.t: Light Trial: lift the black pillar at the entrance with the Golden Gauntlets to get in, then use the Lens of Truth to reveal the invisible path and the hidden Silver Rupees. Collect them, take the correctly marked exit, then Light-Arrow the orb to free the Light Sage.
steps.stuck: Much of this trial is invisible — keep the Lens of Truth up to find the path and the Silver Rupees. Of the exits, take the one the Lens shows as real; the false ones loop you back.

sections.name: The Last Trials & the Great Fairy
sections.sub: Forest, Water, Spirit — and the final upgrade

steps.t: Forest Trial: play the Song of Time to drop a block onto the floor switch, opening the way forward, then work the trial to its end and fire a Light Arrow at the orb to free the Forest Sage, Saria.
Water Trial: scoop Blue Fire into a bottle and use it to melt the red ice blocking the path, collect the Silver Rupees, then Light-Arrow the orb to free the Water Sage, Ruto.
steps.stuck: Only Blue Fire melts the red ice — catch it from the source in an empty bottle, then pour it on the ice walls. Gather all the Silver Rupees to open the door before you can reach the orb.

steps.t: Spirit Trial: bomb the Beamos, use the Longshot to reach a Silver Rupee suspended in mid-air, and play the Sun's Song if you want a healing fairy. Collect what's needed, then Light-Arrow the orb to free the Spirit Sage, Nabooru.

Optional but worth it: step back outside and use the Golden Gauntlets to lift the giant gray pillar near the entrance. In the cave behind it, play Zelda's Lullaby to summon the last Great Fairy — she blesses you so all damage you take is halved.

items.name: Double Defense
items.note: The final Great Fairy's blessing — enemy attacks now deal half damage. Your hearts flash to show it's active.

steps.stuck: Only the Golden Gauntlets can move that pillar, so make this trip after the Shadow Trial. Step into the fountain and play Zelda's Lullaby to call the Great Fairy.

steps.t: With all six orbs destroyed, the barrier sealing the center of the castle vanishes. Head into the heart of the tower — faint organ music drifts down from above, growing louder as you climb.

sections.name: The Climb & Ganondorf
sections.sub: Up the tower to the organ hall

steps.t: Climb the tower. First a room with two Stalfos (a one-minute fight) — beating both makes a chest appear with the Boss Key. Next, a room with a Beamos and two Dinalfos. Then a pair of Iron Knuckles guards the final staircase.

items.name: Boss Key
items.note: Opens the door to Ganondorf's organ hall at the top of the tower.

steps.stuck: Iron Knuckles hit like a truck but move slowly. Wait for the heavy axe swing, sidestep or backflip, then dart in for a slash or two and retreat. You can wake one at a time to fight them one by one.

steps.t: At the top, Ganondorf himself sits at the great organ, Zelda still sealed in his crystal. He rises to fight on a platform high above the ruined castle.

Ganondorf opens with Dead Man's Volley — he hurls a ball of energy you must swat back with your sword. Rally it back and forth, faster each time, until your return overwhelms him and he's left stunned in the air.
steps.stuck: It's a tennis match: time a sword swing as the orb reaches you to send it back faster. He'll speed up — eventually your return is too quick for him and the orb blasts him, leaving him stunned. A spin attack can also bat it back.

steps.t: The instant he's stunned, fire a Light Arrow to bring him crashing down, then jump across and slash him with the Master Sword. Repeat the volley-then-arrow pattern until he falls.
steps.stuck: If he charges a large orb overhead that splits into many, you can't return it — either dodge the spray or shoot him with a Light Arrow during the wind-up. He's only open to your sword right after a Light Arrow knocks him down.

steps.t: Beaten, Ganondorf collapses — but in a last fit of hatred he sets the castle to crumble around you. Zelda's barrier shatters and she is freed. Now you both have to get out.

sections.name: Escape with Zelda
sections.sub: Three minutes before the tower falls

steps.t: The tower is collapsing — you have three minutes. Follow Zelda down through the crumbling halls, watching for falling rocks as you run.
Partway down, Zelda is trapped in a ring of fire and two Stalfos drop in to block you. Beat them and the flames fall, freeing her so you can keep moving.
steps.stuck: Don't panic over the timer — it's generous if you fight fast. Use Z-targeting and jump attacks to drop the two Stalfos; the fire around Zelda vanishes the moment both are dead, then sprint for the exit.

steps.t: Burst out onto the field as the castle finishes collapsing behind you. You think it's over — until the rubble erupts and Ganondorf rises one last time.

sections.name: Ganon
sections.sub: Final boss — the beast of the boar

steps.t: Drawing on the Triforce of Power, Ganondorf transforms into Ganon — a towering boar wielding two great swords. With one blow he knocks the Master Sword from your hand, hurling it beyond a ring of fire.

Ganon's weak point is the glowing tail behind him. Strike him between the eyes with a Light Arrow to stun him and drop the tail into reach, then roll between his legs to get behind him and hit the tail with the Biggoron's Sword or Megaton Hammer.
steps.stuck: A Light Arrow to the face freezes him for a moment — that's your window to roll under or around to the tail. The Master Sword is locked behind fire for now, so any weapon works in this phase; the Biggoron's Sword or Megaton Hammer hit hardest. Low on arrows? As he winds up to attack, roll under his legs and strike the tail anyway.

steps.t: After enough hits to the tail, Ganon reels and the ring of fire briefly drops. Zelda calls out — dash through the gap and grab the Master Sword back into your hand.
steps.stuck: When the flames fall, don't attack — run straight for the gleaming Master Sword and grab it before the fire returns. Only the Master Sword can land the finishing blow.

steps.t: With the Master Sword back, repeat the pattern — Light Arrow to stun, roll behind, and carve his tail. This time you must use the Master Sword for the final hits; the Biggoron's Sword can't finish him.

When Ganon finally drops, Zelda uses her power as a Sage to hold him fast. Walk up and drive the Master Sword home for the finishing blow — and the six Sages seal the King of Evil away in the Sacred Realm.
Peace returns to Hyrule. Zelda thanks you, then plays the Ocarina of Time to send you back to your stolen childhood — so you can live the years Ganondorf took. Roll credits. You did it.

RUNES.name: Kokiri Sword
RUNES.from: The training-ground maze behind the rock-blocked hole in Kokiri Forest.
RUNES.what: Your first blade as a child. Crawl through the hole and cross the maze, dodging the rolling boulder, to find the chest.
RUNES.tip: Roll (A while running) to move fast and break the boulder's timing. You keep this sword until you draw the Master Sword.

RUNES.name: Deku Shield
RUNES.from: Bought from the Kokiri Shop for 40 Rupees (cut grass and lift rocks for the money).
RUNES.what: A wooden shield you hold up with R. Needed to deflect the Deku Tree's webs and Deku Scrub seeds.
RUNES.tip: It burns if a flame touches it — buy a spare. Mido won't let you into the Deku Tree until you have BOTH a sword and a shield.

RUNES.name: Fairy Slingshot
RUNES.from: A chest inside the Great Deku Tree.
RUNES.what: Fires Deku Seeds at distant targets, switches, eyes, and the ladder-cord. Your first ranged weapon.
RUNES.tip: Hold ZR/Z to aim in first person. Shoot Skulltulas in the eye and hit the gold eye-switches to drop ladders.

RUNES.name: Fairy Ocarina
RUNES.from: Saria gives it to you on the bridge as you leave Kokiri Forest.
RUNES.what: Play songs to trigger magic — open doors, warp, change the time of day, summon your horse. The heart of the game.
RUNES.tip: Later swapped for the Ocarina of Time. Learn songs from people you meet; the notes appear on screen as you're taught.

RUNES.name: Zelda's Lullaby
RUNES.from: Impa teaches it behind Hyrule Castle after you meet Princess Zelda.
RUNES.what: The royal family's song. Opens royal-crest doors and gates, and makes Gossip Stones and Triforce switches react.
RUNES.tip: Many puzzles across the game ask for this song on a Triforce mark — keep it handy.

RUNES.name: Bombs
RUNES.from: Bomb Flowers in Dodongo's Cavern and on Death Mountain; later a Bomb Bag from the Dodongo's Cavern area.
RUNES.what: Blow open cracked walls and boulders, stun enemies, and drop them in monster mouths. Essential for progress.
RUNES.tip: Pick a Bomb Flower (it regrows) if you're out of bombs. Throw, don't hold — they explode on a timer.

CHAMPIONS.name: Kokiri's Emerald
CHAMPIONS.note: The Forest's Spiritual Stone — the Great Deku Tree entrusts it to you after Queen Gohma falls.

CHAMPIONS.name: Goron's Ruby
CHAMPIONS.note: The Fire Spiritual Stone — Darunia gives it once you clear the Gorons' cavern of Dodongos.

CHAMPIONS.name: Zora's Sapphire
CHAMPIONS.note: The Water Spiritual Stone — Princess Ruto hands it to you after Barinade is defeated.

CHAMPIONS.name: Light Medallion
CHAMPIONS.note: Rauru, the Sage of Light, grants it when you awaken as an adult in the Chamber of Sages.

CHAMPIONS.name: Forest Medallion
CHAMPIONS.note: Saria becomes the Sage of Forest after you defeat Phantom Ganon.

CHAMPIONS.name: Fire Medallion
CHAMPIONS.note: Darunia becomes the Sage of Fire after Volvagia is slain.

CHAMPIONS.name: Water Medallion
CHAMPIONS.note: Ruto becomes the Sage of Water after Morpha falls.

CHAMPIONS.name: Shadow Medallion
CHAMPIONS.note: Impa becomes the Sage of Shadow after Bongo Bongo is beaten.

CHAMPIONS.name: Spirit Medallion
CHAMPIONS.note: Nabooru becomes the Sage of Spirit after Twinrova is defeated.

ROADMAP.name: Pieces of Heart
ROADMAP.note: 36 scattered across Hyrule in minigames, chests, and hidden corners. Every four you collect add a full heart.

ROADMAP.name: Gold Skulltulas
ROADMAP.note: Slay golden Skulltulas and collect their tokens; turn them in to the cursed family in Kakariko for rewards.

ROADMAP.name: Biggoron's Sword
ROADMAP.note: A long chain of timed trades up Death Mountain ends in the strongest two-handed sword in the game.

ROADMAP.name: Every Ocarina Song
ROADMAP.note: Warp songs, weather songs, and the storytelling melodies — collecting them all unlocks fast travel and secrets.

TIPS.name: Getting started
items: Z-target (or L) to lock onto enemies and people — it's how you aim, strafe, and read what to do next.
Cut every bush and lift every rock for Rupees, and check behind waterfalls and in odd corners for secrets.
Save often (and note: dying sends you back to the dungeon entrance or your last save, not the title).

TIPS.name: Child & Adult Link
items: Pulling the Master Sword jumps you 7 years forward; later you can travel between child and adult freely at the Temple of Time.
Some puzzles need the OTHER age — a bean planted as a child grows into a platform for the adult, for example.
The Sun's Song flips day and night instantly; some enemies (Stalchildren, ReDeads) and shops depend on the time.
